mnemonics: I Search For Max Min Count (ISFMMC): Imagine Inserting a node, then Searching for it, Finding its Max and Min values, and Counting all Nodes to track it.
keyPoints: Insertion in a BST involves traversing the tree to find the correct null location based on value comparison.
Searching uses the BST property: go left for smaller values, right for larger ones.
The maximum value is found by traversing right until null; the minimum by traversing left until null.
Counting nodes requires a traversal (e.g., in-order) that visits and increments a counter for each node.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update parent pointers during insertion, which can break the tree structure.
Assuming a balanced tree during search, leading to incorrect O(log n) time complexity estimates for worst-case (O(n)).
Not handling edge cases like an empty tree when finding min/max or counting nodes, which can cause null pointer errors.
